clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.
The Service Provider shall develop the mobile application described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] for [PROJECT_NAME], targeting [TARGET_PLATFORMS].
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [DELIVERY_DATE] at , subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.

The Client shall test the application against [DEVICE_OS_COMPATIBILITY_MATRIX] using [ACCEPTANCE_METHOD] within [ACCEPTANCE_PERIOD_DAYS] days of delivery. If the Client does not respond within this period, the application shall be deemed accepted.

Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Service Provider assigns to the Client all rights, title, and interest in the custom source code of the application, under the ownership model [IP_OWNERSHIP_MODEL].

Where the Client requests App Store submission, the Service Provider shall prepare and submit the application, but shall not be liable for delay, rejection, or removal resulting from the App Store's independent review process, which is outside either Party's control.

Where the Client requests Google Play submission, the Service Provider shall prepare and submit the application, but shall not be liable for delay, rejection, or removal resulting from Google Play's independent review process, which is outside either Party's control.

Where [MAINTENANCE_INCLUDED] indicates inclusion, the Service Provider shall provide maintenance services for [MAINTENANCE_PERIOD] following launch, for a fee of [MAINTENANCE_FEE] per [BILLING_PERIOD].
Where the application integrates [ANALYTICS_PROVIDER], the Client acknowledges that user data may be processed by that third-party provider under its own terms, separate from this Agreement.

Where the application integrates [CRASH_MONITORING_PROVIDER], the Client acknowledges that diagnostic data may be processed by that third-party provider under its own terms, separate from this Agreement.
Where the Services include training, the Service Provider shall provide [TRAINING_HOURS] hours of training on managing the application.
Where the Client requests technical support beyond the Warranty Clause period, the Service Provider shall provide it at [SUPPORT_HOURLY_RATE] per hour.
The application shall be built using [TECHNOLOGY_STACK].
The Services shall be performed using [DEVELOPMENT_METHODOLOGY] methodology, with milestone reviews conducted accordingly.
The project shall proceed according to the milestones described in [MILESTONE_SCHEDULE], with payment released per the Payment Clause upon completion of each milestone.
The Service Provider shall perform quality assurance testing covering [QA_TEST_SCOPE] before delivering each milestone.
Each Party retains ownership of intellectual property it owned before this Agreement, including any framework, library, or SDK the Service Provider incorporates into the application.

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.
Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, or consequential loss arising from this Agreement.
Each Party's total liability arising from this Agreement shall not exceed [TOTAL_VALUE], except for loss arising from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. | Field Category | Field Name | Type | Allowed Values / Format | |---|---|---|---| | Party Role | [CLIENT_ROLE] | enum | Client, Buyer, Licensee | | Party Role | [PROVIDER_ROLE] | enum | Developer, Service Provider, Contractor | | Payment Method | [PAYMENT_METHOD] | enum | Bank Transfer, Credit Card, PayPal, Wire Transfer, Check | | Currency | [CURRENCY] | enum (ISO 4217) | USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, AUD, NZD, BRL | | Delivery Method | [DELIVERY_METHOD] | enum | Email, Cloud Storage Link, Git Repository, Physical Media, Deployment to Production | | Licensing Model | [LICENSE_TYPE] | enum | Exclusive, Non-Exclusive, Perpetual, Time-Limited | | Revision Model | [REVISION_MODEL] | enum | Fixed Rounds, Time-Boxed, Unlimited-Within-Scope | | Acceptance Method | [ACCEPTANCE_METHOD] | enum | Deemed Acceptance (silence), Explicit Written Approval, Formal Sign-off Document | | Cancellation Reason | [CANCELLATION_REASON] | enum | Client Convenience, Non-Payment, Breach, Force Majeure | | Termination Reason | [TERMINATION_REASON] | enum | For Convenience, For Cause, Non-Payment, Chronic SLA Failure, Mutual Agreement | | Intellectual Property Option | [IP_OWNERSHIP_MODEL] | enum | Full Assignment to Client, License to Client with Provider Retaining Ownership, Joint Ownership | | Confidentiality Level | [CONFIDENTIALITY_LEVEL] | enum | Public, Internal, Confidential, Highly Confidential/Trade Secret | | Notice Method | [NOTICE_METHOD] | enum | Email, Written Letter, Platform Notification, Registered Mail | | Time Unit | [TIME_UNIT] | enum | Hours, Days, Weeks, Months, Years | | Percentage Field | [PERCENTAGE_FIELDS] | number (0-100) | Applies to cancellation fees, service credits, revenue shares | | Quantity Field | [QUANTITY_FIELDS] | integer | Applies to screens, pages, revisions, users, hours | | Boolean Option | [BOOLEAN_FIELDS] | boolean | true/false — applies to inclusion flags (e.g., SOURCE_FILES_INCLUDED, DRONE_REQUIRED) | Note: Each Contract Type below inherits this baseline schema and adds Contract-Type-specific enumerations inline within its own Structured Fields subsection. | Field | Type | Allowed Values | |---|---|---| | [TARGET_PLATFORMS] | multi-select enum | iOS, Android, Cross-Platform | | [DEVELOPMENT_METHODOLOGY] | enum | Agile, Waterfall, Hybrid | | [MAINTENANCE_INCLUDED] | boolean | true, false | | [ACCEPTANCE_METHOD] | enum | Deemed Acceptance, Explicit Written Approval, Formal Sign-off Document | | [IP_OWNERSHIP_MODEL] | enum | Full Assignment to Client, License to Client | | [BILLING_PERIOD] | enum | Monthly, Quarterly, Annually | Ambiguity: none — device/OS compatibility explicitly enumerated as a Dynamic Field matrix rather than a vague "compatible with modern devices" phrase. Duplication: App Store Publication and Google Play Publication kept as separate Conditional Blocks despite similar wording, since each store's review process is independently triggered and legally distinct. Loophole closed: both store-publication disclaimers explicitly attribute delay/rejection risk to the platform's independent process, not the developer, closing Step 2 risk #1. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: Analytics and Crash Monitoring Clauses added, disclosing third-party data processing absent from Website Development given mobile's heavier reliance on SDKs. Complexity: reused Source Code Ownership and Maintenance patterns from Website Development for consistency, adapted only where mobile-specific risk required (Required rather than Conditional for Source Code Ownership since mobile apps are rarely built on off-the-shelf templates the way websites can be). Enforceability: acceptance testing tied to structured compatibility matrix, enabling automated database representation. Attack: Client blames developer for a 3-week Apple review rejection delay — mitigated by explicit App Store Publication disclaimer. Attack: Client discovers user data flows to a third-party analytics provider without prior disclosure — mitigated by Analytics Clause's explicit acknowledgment requirement. Attack: Client disputes acceptance claiming the app doesn't work on an untested old OS version — mitigated by explicit compatibility matrix defining the tested scope. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
